rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydroboration of styrenes with HBpin using Pt electrodes, nBu4NBF4 electrolyte and DIEA in CH3CN/THF
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CH3CN and/or HBpin generates radical cations that, after deprotonation, yield .CH2CN and .Bpin radicals; the .Bpin radical adds to the styrene, and the resulting benzylic C-radical is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that is protonated by DIEA-H+ (or THF-H+) to give the hydroboration product
electron_flow: At the anode, CH3CN (and/or HBpin, or DIEA when present) is oxidized by SET to a radical cation; deprotonation/H-atom loss gives .CH2CN and .Bpin radicals. .Bpin adds to the terminal carbon of styrene to give a benzylic radical, which is reduced at the cathode to a carbanion and protonated by [DIEA-H]+/[THF-H]+ to deliver the anti-Markovnikov hydroboration product, while H+ reduction at the cathode releases H2.

probe_reagents.name: THF-d8 (solvent label)
probe_reagents.observation: No deuterium incorporation into 3a, indicating THF is not the proton source for the benzylic position under standard conditions

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN (solvent label)
probe_reagents.observation: Partial D incorporation at benzylic position, d1-3a:3a = 84:16 (47% yield), implicating CH3CN as the proton/H source for the benzylic C-H

probe_reagents.name: d8-styrene
probe_reagents.observation: 1H NMR shows a single new H at the benzylic methine (1.22 ppm), confirming the H delivered to benzylic carbon comes from an external proton source (CH3CN/DIEA), Bpin goes to terminal CH2

probe_reagents.name: galvinoxyl radical
probe_reagents.observation: Galvinoxyl-Bpin adduct (8) detected by MS, supporting formation of .Bpin radical

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: EPR shows DMPO-H adduct under standard conditions; in absence of HBpin or styrene, DMPO-CH2CN (10) is also observed, confirming .CH2CN radical formation by anodic oxidation of CH3CN

probe_reagents.name: H2 detection by GC
probe_reagents.observation: H2 evolution monitored by SHIMADZU GC-2014 in various omission experiments, consistent with cathodic proton reduction

notes: Two mechanistic cycles are proposed: (i) standard conditions with DIEA, where anodic SET on DIEA gives [DIEA]+. that mediates generation of .Bpin and .CH2CN; the benzylic radical from .Bpin addition to styrene is reduced cathodically and protonated by [DIEA-H]+; (ii) in the absence of DIEA, direct anodic oxidation of CH3CN and HBpin produces the same radicals, with THF/[THF2-H]+ acting as proton shuttle. A separate cycle for diboration (with 2-vinylnaphthalene, 7r) shows that a second .Bpin radical traps the benzylic C-radical before cathodic reduction; control experiments rule out B2pin2 involvement, so the diboronate ester is not formed from hydroboration product.
